author: m.kalinin
content:
Doesn't the accumulator need to commit to `prev_randao`, `gas_limit`, `block_hash`, `execution_requests`, `slot_number`, `parent_hash`, `timestamp`, `withdrawals` and all potential future fields which values are validated by CL?

Basically, any payload attribute that is secured by CL need to be there. Today, each payload field is secured and thus full `block_hash` validation is required for each block.

With Gloas the situation changes and only a subset of payload fields is CL verifiable. It's not necessarily should be an accumulator, a commitment on those fields that can be validated by EL and CL would be enough. Today, CL that can validated `block_hash` on their own can securely range sync without sending payloads to EL. Maybe, this is the way to go? Make all CL's run `block_hash` validation instead of introducing a new commitment that is understood by both sides?

author: m.kalinin
content:
Another question I want us to discuss before making such a change is how the range sync will look like with stateless/zk EL?

Getting rid of payload range requests means that in the future nodes will keep relying on EL p2p even when optimistic sync can be deprecated which kind of the opposite to the thinking that we had at the merge time, where there was a strong desire to get rid of block requests in p2p. Though, getting rid of it might be unfeasible due to many reasons.

The discussion points:
- are there any CL relying on payload range requests today?
- will the simplification we are making today become an obstacle in the future?

author: potuz
content: The point is not downloading the block

author: potuz
content: If the hash is guaranteed then all elements covered by the hash would be fine isn't it?

author: potuz
content: And no, nodes won't ever need the Payload so they don't need to rely on the EL at all

author: nero_eth
content:
I agree with <@425572898787426305> , great catch there!
Also the accumulator seems like a nice solution but have to review it in more depth yet.
Two things I didn't get after initial review:
The accumulator uses block_hash as input but the request hash is part of the hash, wouldn't this cause a circular dependency? I think we could defer the request hash by one block on the EL if needes, or use the parent_block_hash.
Also, why start with Hash32() at fork boundary? Or will there be a different initial accumulater at the fork?

author: potuz
content: Yes Misha is right that the accumulator should be over everything. I don't think there's a circular dependency if the accumulator is not part of the hash.

author: potuz
content: Starting from zero is not a problem I believe?

author: m.kalinin
content: If we don't want to start from zero it might be some magic number, like the hash of the fork_version

author: nero_eth
content: Right but on the EL side, I guess we would want to put the accumulator into the EL block header?

author: nero_eth
content:
So, (1) either we don't put it into the EL block header, or (2) we except it when calculating the block hash (we have not done that before), or (3) we use the parent_block_hash.
I'd prefer option 3.

author: m.kalinin
content:
So, the concept we will introduce is something as the following:
```
PartialBlockHeader:
    parent_hash: Bytes32
    ... # commitments to all other fields meaningful to CL
```

`hash(partial_block_header)` will become a part of EL's block and will also be `block_hashed` like any other regular field. `parent_hash` will serve as accumulator as the previous block contains `hash(partial_block_header)` as well

author: m.kalinin
content: the `PartialBlockHeader` name is just for illustration

author: m.kalinin
content: I see, we would need some commitment to the current withdrawals to be incorporated into accumulator. And that commitment should be verifiable by EL. If we use previous withdrawals then they seem to be already incorporated by the `parent_hash`?

author: potuz
content: well but they need to be verified by the CL

author: potuz
content: to match the withdrawals, otherwise it' s the same for all other fields, we can just not include them if they are part of the hash

author: potuz
content: but the problem is that the CL does not verify the hash

author: m.kalinin
content: ok, worth more thinking into this

author: potuz
content: in fact we can add the current withdrawals to the accumulator, and not include the accumulator in the bid, but rather in the next block as the requests are included

author: potuz
content: the payload can still be verified in gossip since the accumulator can be computed anyway offchain
